description:
fixtures/multi_clause.fz exercises a guard clause (`fn classify(n) when n > 0, do: :positive`). Today fz run errors with:

  error[lower/unsupported]: unsupported: guards (deferred)
  --> fixtures/multi_clause.fz:2:1

The historic ticket fz-ul4.12.5 ("Multi-clause + guards + tuple patterns + self-TCO") closed against the pre-IR direct-style backend (fz-ul4.11.9 retired that path). Guards never landed in ir_lower.

## Deliverable

ir_lower passes guards through to fz_ir; ir_codegen lowers them as a per-clause precondition test before the clause body executes; on guard-fail, fall through to the next clause. Honor pattern bindings inside the guard expression.

## Acceptance

- fixtures/multi_clause.fz runs cleanly via `fz run` and produces:
    :zero
    :positive
    :negative
    120
- Test added to fixture_matrix as `# paths: jit` (graduates from deferred).
title: Guard clauses () in fz-IR lowering
